prompt: Which of these formulas is a tautology (true in every row of its truth table)?
hint: A tautology can never be false. Build the truth table and look for a column of all T.
explanation: P ∨ ¬P (the law of excluded middle) is true whether P is true or false, so it is a tautology. P ∧ ¬P is a contradiction (always false); P → ¬P and P ↔ ¬P are each false in at least one row, so they are contingent or contradictory, not tautologies.
